Practical help for small-space growers

Urban gardening advice for balconies, windowsills, patios, and indoor growing.

Find clear, practical guidance for herbs, vegetables, hydroponics, grow lights, and container growing that matches real homes, real light, and realistic maintenance.

Explore guides Start here
Practical testing Advice stays focused on the changes that actually improve results in small spaces.
Clear recommendations Straight answers on what is worth buying, what is optional, and what to skip.
Easy to follow Guides start with the basics so you can solve the real problem first.
What you'll find here

Advice for real homes, not fantasy setups.

The site follows the way most growing problems unfold: choose the spot, check the light, pick the right container, then troubleshoot if something starts slipping.

Start here

A calm path for new growers.

Begin with space, light, container size, and crop choice before spending on extra gear or complicated routines.

Grow Lights March 25, 2026

LED vs. Fluorescent Grow Lights for Herbs: Which is Better?

Fluorescent tubes used to be the gold standard for indoor gardeners, but modern LEDs have changed the game. Find out which technology is right for your space and budget.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Hydroponics March 25, 2026

When Should You Change Hydroponic Nutrient Solution? Top-Off vs. Full Reset

You can't just keep adding water to a hydroponic system forever. Eventually, the chemical balance becomes a toxic soup. Here is the exact schedule to follow for topping off versus doing a full reservoir reset.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Plant Problems March 25, 2026

Why Is My Basil Wilting Indoors? Stop the Droop

Basil is famous for being dramatic. If your plant suddenly wilts into a sad green puddle, don't panic. Here is exactly how to diagnose the droop and revive it.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
First principles

Build a growing system you can maintain through ordinary weeks.

Good beginner advice reduces complexity before it adds options. The most reliable early wins come from honest light assessment, root space, and consistent watering.

  • Match crops to your real light conditions, not your ideal plan.
  • Prioritize root volume and drainage before chasing fertilizers.
  • Keep watering routines boring and consistent.
  • Use simple systems you can maintain through busy weeks.
Popular guides

Popular guides readers return to often.

These are strong starting points if you want practical guidance before getting into narrower questions.

Urban Gardening March 20, 2026

How Often Should You Water Potted Herbs? (Stop Guessing)

The most common way beginners kill potted herbs is by watering them on a strict calendar schedule. Throw away the calendar and learn the 'weight test' instead.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Hydroponics March 1, 2026

Hydroponic Nutrients for Beginners: EC and pH Without the Confusion

Most hydroponic problems are not mysterious. They come from concentration drift, pH drift, heat, or neglected maintenance. This guide covers the basics clearly.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Soil & Fertilizers February 26, 2026

Potting Mix vs Raised Bed Soil: Fix Container Yellowing

Container plants need air space, drainage, and predictable structure. That is why raised bed soil and in-ground blends usually disappoint in pots.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Grow Lights February 22, 2026

How Many Hours Should Grow Lights Run for Herbs?

A longer light schedule is not always a better one. Herbs need enough daily light, but they also benefit from a stable dark period and realistic fixture placement.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide

North-Facing Window Herbs: What Actually Works and When You Need a Grow Light

Why readers trust the site

Clear advice for limited light, limited time, and ordinary budgets.

The goal is simple: make growing easier to understand, easier to troubleshoot, and easier to keep up with during normal life.

01

Practical testing

Recommendations stay focused on setups that are repeatable, useful, and realistic at home.

02

Clear recommendations

Each guide explains what matters, what is optional, and where the real tradeoffs are.

03

Beginner-friendly guidance

Technical ideas are broken into simple decisions for balconies, windowsills, and indoor shelves.

Stay in the loop

Get new guide alerts or send a topic request.

Email is the easiest way to suggest a topic, flag a correction, or ask about an upcoming guide.
